input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-11T01:01:53.511Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to supply a value per theme:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

When the OS theme changes, every `AppThemeBinding` re-evaluates automatically — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
var page = this;

// SetAppThemeColor is a shortcut for Color properties
page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

var title = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
title.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For non-Color properties, use the generic SetAppTheme<T>
var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
button.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Automatic** | Bindings react to OS theme changes at runtime — no restart needed |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` (colors, images, strings, etc.) |
| **Detection** | Read current theme via `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark`) |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme |

For larger apps, move colors into a `ResourceDictionary` with `AppThemeBinding` so every page shares the same palette:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
</Application.Resources>

<!-- Any page -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a tutorial/explanation on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to support light/dark theme switching. This is a knowledge question — they want XAML examples and C# alternatives. I don't need to search a codebase; I can answer directly.
